title: Southern buttermilk pie
subtitle: Custard pie with a clean, tangy filling and flaky pastry base
excerpt: A soft egg custard made with buttermilk, butter, and vanilla baked in a blind-baked pastry shell. A Southern American classic with a gentle tang and a smooth, barely-set filling similar to chess pie.
sourceNotes: Buttermilk pie is a Southern American custard pie that became widespread in the nineteenth century as a farmhouse dessert, using buttermilk left over from churning butter. The recipe appears in American domestic cookery collections from the mid-nineteenth century onward. It is closely related to chess pie (which uses a higher sugar ratio and sometimes cornmeal) but distinguished by its reliance on buttermilk for both tanginess and structure.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled. Thaw overnight in the fridge.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps refrigerated for 3 days. Serve at room temperature.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: 180°C conventional / 160°C fan for the filling; blind-bake pastry at 200°C

content.text: Buttermilk pie has a straightforward filling: eggs, sugar, butter, buttermilk, flour, and vanilla, whisked together and poured into a blind-baked shell. The buttermilk provides both the tang and the acid that helps the custard set at a lower temperature than a cream custard, giving a softer, lighter texture. The filling will puff slightly in the oven and then settle flat as it cools.
Make and blind bake the shell

Make a shortcrust pastry from 200 g {{plain-flour}}, 100 g cold {{unsalted-butter}}, {{fine-salt}}, and 2-3 tbsp cold water. Chill for 30 minutes, then roll out and line a 23 cm pie dish. Trim and chill for 20 minutes. Heat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan). Line the pastry with baking paper and baking beans and blind bake for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper and bake for 5 more minutes. Reduce the oven to 180°C (160°C fan).

Whisk together the {{whole-eggs}}, {{caster-sugar}}, melted {{unsalted-butter}}, {{buttermilk}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{vanilla-extract}}, and {{lemon-juice}} until smooth and well combined. Pour into the warm blind-baked shell. Bake for 35-40 minutes until the edges are set and the centre has just a slight wobble. The filling will set completely as it cools. Cool in the dish for at least 1 hour before slicing.
